old_claim.note: Iatrochemistry (Paracelsus) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Fire assay used fluxes, lead collection, cupellation, and nitric-acid parting, so earlier practical chemistry is an enabling capability for the scoped assay workshop.

source_shape.source_locator: The Fire Assaying page describes Agricola's 1556 method as adding ore-specific fluxes, melting the ore and flux mixture in an assay furnace, cupellation, and nitric-acid parting.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source presents fire assay as a chemical ore-testing process using fluxes, lead collection, cupellation, and acid parting.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That method boundary supports practical chemistry as the enabling capability and makes Paracelsian medical iatrochemistry unnecessary for this metallurgical assay scope.

ontology_before: Chemical Assay Laboratories depended on Iatrochemistry (Paracelsus), mixing medical chemical reform with ore-assay workshop practice.
ontology_after: Metallurgical Fire Assay Workshops depend on earlier practical chemistry for fluxing, cupellation, and parting while excluding Paracelsian medicine from the direct prerequisite set.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: chemical_assay_laboratories now depends on basic_chemistry and no longer directly depends on iatrochemistry_paracelsus.
would_reject_if: The node were broadened back to medical iatrochemical laboratories rather than metallurgical fire-assay workshops.
A source showed Agricola-style fire assay specifically required Paracelsian medical doctrine.
The basic_chemistry node were later narrowed to exclude fluxing, cupellation, acid parting, and practical material processing.
short_reason: Fire assay is practical chemical ore testing, not a Paracelsian medical-chemistry laboratory.
